mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance against zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and contraction in consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without overheating, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline when either series moves independently, or IC drops below 0.01 over 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects capacity utilization gains and margin expansion, yielding positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle expansions, or 5-day forward returns show negative alpha when INDPRO growth exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses 3.0% annualized, central bank tightening expectations rise and real discount rates increase, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally despite inflation breaching 3.0%, or the threshold shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and real GDP growth signal synchronized economic expansion and credit availability, driving positive equity market performance over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when housing starts rise while GDP growth stagnates, or interaction term loses significance in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal immediate supply chain contraction and earnings downgrades, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Short-term equity returns remain positive during consecutive industrial production declines, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 1-year windows.
